rationale: The cited paper is a well-known social-transmission/word-of-mouth antecedent study, and the citing paper also studies sharing behavior (referrals) and a psychological mechanism, so it could plausibly be a foundational theory source. However, the only evidence provided is a DOI-level match with no in-text citation context, and the focal constructs differ (content virality/emotional arousal vs. referral contagion/social appropriateness). Given this sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop but not strong enough to clearly keep.
